programmes.show_description: Paramedics treat a young man who's collapsed outside a nightclub and isn't breathing, a young woman who's having a sickle cell crisis and a woman with crippling chest pains.
King's College Hospital in London has one of Britain's busiest A&E departments. In this episode, two cyclists arrive in A&E, and a retired docker takes a shine to nurse Laura.
This episode focuses on some of the youngest patients treated by King's College Hospital A&E, and meets 27-year-old emergency medicine trainee Matt.
More favourite investigations. Kate and Helen get the scoop on vanilla. In the Netherlands, Matt explores the myth that Edam balls were once used as emergency cannon balls!
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Stroud. The first host, horse trainer Melanie, opts for a quick and easy, Italian-inspired vegetarian menu so she can focus on a glamorous night of hosting... and partying! (Ep1/5)
Stroud. Second host Elliot has made a good impression on one of his guests, but others are annoyed by simmering dinner table tensions and boastful claims - about livestock. (Ep2/5)
Stroud. Belarusian Tatsy serves up a taste of her culture and heritage complete with Russian dancing and pretend figure skating. But will guest grumbles leave her night on thin ice? (Ep3/5)
Stroud. Marketing executive Liv serves up a menu inspired by Italy, despite having never been there! Will her arancini and homemade ravioli make up for her rather lumpy panna cotta? (Ep4/5)
Stroud. There's rice rivalry on the final night, as radio presenter Kate serves up a risotto similar to Mel's. But can Kate's magical table decoration bag her the grand? (Ep5/5)
Andi and Neil dream of retiring to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Can Laura Hamilton find their perfect place close to the coastal town of Mazarrón, near Murcia, for £145,000?
Dawn and Ian want to fulfil their dream of owning a house in Tavira in Portugal. For £250k, will any Algarve offerings impress them enough to make an offer?
Harriet wants a holiday home in the popular resort of Villamartin on Spain's Costa Blanca. Property expert Lee Juggurnauth finds five fabulous homes for her budget of £110,000.
Uri and his wife Hazel are keen to find a permanent home in south west Cyprus, to escape the cold Scottish winters. Can Leah Charles-King find the perfect place for their £200k?
Portugal's Algarve is a very special place for Audrey and Tony, and they would love to buy their very own holiday home there. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £220k can buy.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Farhall family in Windsor. Nardeth is fed up with the layout of their four-bed detached home, but Graham loves the location.
Kirstie house-hunts with a pair of nervous first-time buyers in Balham and Putney in south west London. Phil looks for a big, Aussie-style property in the Thames Valley.
Na'im and Sara host first at their hotel in the breathtaking Cumbrian countryside. But one pair are too preoccupied with the furniture to care about the view.
Neil and Jack host at their hotel near majestic Windermere, where the B&Bers enjoy yet more stunning views. But there's still negativity in the feedback.
At Chris's glampsite in Richmond, North Yorkshire, off-grid really does mean off the electricity grid. Which is quite a worry for some of the B&Bers...
For the final visit the B&Bers go glamping again, at Phil and Jane's site in Derbyshire, where the stag-gering views include real-life deer wandering about the place.
On payment day, the B&Bers clash over furniture failings and clock complaints. And sparks fly - ironically - over a lack of electricity. Who's won?
Brand new series: Paramedics treat a man having a heart attack who thinks he's just pulled a muscle. And a day out hiking ends badly for a man who's fallen and injured his ankle.
This episode meets people who've taken big risks and explores how it's affected their lives. Motorbikers James and Peter are both rushed in. Ex-miner Alfred has severe chest pain.
Keith is rushed to Queen's after a cardiac arrest. Joanna has a spinal fracture after falling off a horse. And 79-year-old John has been run over by a car while out shopping.
Paramedics treat a woman with a possible bleed on the brain, a baby whose heart is beating extremely fast, a man showing signs of a stroke, and a woman who may have sepsis.
This episode meets people who've taken big risks and explores how it's affected their lives. Motorbikers James and Peter are both rushed in. Ex-miner Alfred has severe chest pain.
Keith is rushed to Queen's after a cardiac arrest. Joanna has a spinal fracture after falling off a horse. And 79-year-old John has been run over by a car while out shopping.
A selection of favourite investigations. Kate Quilton visits McVitie's to ask why biscuits have little holes all over them. Jimmy Doherty finds out how wine bottle corks are made.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The campsite critics begin their north Wales adventures at Porth Iago campsite on the Llyn Peninsula. Will they be won over by some beachside beer tasting and early-morning yoga?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien fix up and sell unwanted items to make a profit for their owners. In Wiltshire, Henry gives Simon a motorcycling masterclass.
Married couple Mo and Anthony are looking for a holiday apartment with sea views in Torremolinos on Spain's Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £130k budget.
Glasgow. The first host, part-time princess and full-time hairdresser Jen, hopes that her pink-themed night of no-nonsense grub and good fun can bag her the grand. (Ep1/5)
Glasgow. Psychic medium Robert plans to wow his guests with his Turkish wedding-inspired menu and a smattering of spirituality. But is it enough to put him in the top spot? (Ep2/5)
Glasgow. Food vlogger Darren serves up deep-fried delights, including lasagne, lettuce and chocolate bars. Are his guests in battered bliss, or is it too much for some? (Ep3/5)
Glasgow. Partnerships manager Dorine goes all out with glitter and gold, and a French-inspired menu sprinkled with flavours of her childhood. But tensions rise among her guests... (Ep4/5)
Glasgow. Nail technician Kasia hopes that her traditional Polish food and lively entertainment will impress her guests - after she spent the week criticising their food! (Ep5/5)
The first visit is to Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages in County Durham, run by Tony and Viv Smith. Their guests love the views, pool and hot tubs, but will they pay top dollar for their stay?
John and Leslie host at The Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire. After a visit to a model village, will their guests' mixed reviews leave a bitter taste?
Jane and Rachel welcome their rivals to Stoneleigh House Hotel in Buckingham. Things seem to be going well - until they forget their guests' names...
The final visit is to Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, run by Howard and Amanda Wooliams. Everyone is glad to be glamping, but the feedback leads to questions.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
BMW 2002 Turbo: In the West Midlands, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue one of Tim's all-time favourites: a BMW 2002 Turbo, which has been rusting away on its owner's driveway.
Vauxhall Astra GTE: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to South Wales to rescue former boy racer Huw's rusty Vauxhall Astra GTE, which has been off the road for over 20 years.
Three-year-old Ollie and his family search for a forever friend; is Staffy puppy Tiger the one? And can Frankie the pup help Simon overcome a dog attack trauma?
In Derbyshire, Mike and Sarah set out to build a multigenerational longhouse. But with a complicated roof, a shortage of materials and an unexpected bereavement, it proves far from easy...
...Clive and Kids: After a wet and stormy autumn leaves the project months behind schedule, the new year brings new cheer, as the build hits a major milestone.
This episode explores the ways in which men deal with pain and injury, from a cyst on the knee to a dislocated shoulder that takes a five-strong team to pull back into place.
Nineteen-year-old fitness fanatic Josh is flown to King's after being injured in a canal barge accident. And two best friends are recovering from a Sunday night out clubbing.
